_meta.method: Web research across Buddhistisk Samfund, Zenbuddhistisk Forening, Boundless Way Zen, One Drop Zen, Big Heart Zen, Interbeing Denmark, and Wikipedia/BuddhaNet directories
_meta.coverageEstimate: ~85% of active groups; Denmark has ~5-8 known Zen groups per Wikipedia 'Buddhism in Denmark' (~100 practitioners total)
knownGaps: Sanbo Zen Danmark not located as a distinct Danish group; appears to be subsumed within German/Swedish Sanbo Zen presence
Specific street address for Boundless Way Zen Copenhagen (Frederiksberg) not published
Aarhus Zendo (Øsal Ling) is primarily Tibetan/Dzogchen with Zen overlap; included as hybrid
Bovbjerg Sōtō Zen Center referenced in prompt but no online presence found - may be defunct or never existed
Some sangha sub-groups (Wake Up etc.) may operate informally without standalone web presence

places.name: Havredal Zendo (Buddhistisk Samfund)
places.notes: First Zen temple building in Denmark, founded by Choan Denko Mokudo Bertelsen; current leadership Shuho Seishin Sommerhall; HQ of Buddhistisk Samfund (buddhistisksamfund.dk)

places.name: Zen-Buddhistisk Forening (Hokori Sangha)
places.notes: Independent group with affiliation to Sogenji monastery and Shodo Harada Roshi; meets in central Copenhagen by arrangement

places.name: One Drop Zendo København
places.lineage: Rinzai (Shodo Harada Roshi / Sogenji / One Drop Zen)
places.notes: Contact Peter Daiko Skovgaard; meets Sundays 17:30-19:30 at FOF facilities (currently displaced after building fire)

places.name: One Drop Zendo Odense (Ten Chi Dojo)
places.lineage: Rinzai (Shodo Harada Roshi / Sogenji / One Drop Zen)
places.notes: Practicing zazen since 2001; meets first Wednesday of month 18:00; connection to Shodo Harada Roshi and One Drop Zendo community

places.name: Boundless Way Zen Copenhagen
places.lineage: Soto/Harada-Yasutani koan curriculum + Korean Linji (Boundless Way Zen)
places.notes: Led by Esther Sorgenfrei (Dharma Holder, Denkai Aug 2025), student of David Dae An Rynick Roshi and Melissa Moyzen Blacker Roshi; weekly Monday evening practice

places.name: Big Heart Zen Copenhagen
places.lineage: Kanzeon Sangha / White Plum / Genpo Roshi lineage
places.notes: Led by Birgitte Myoin, ordained Zen Monk and long-term student of Soten Genpo Roshi; donation-based, online and in-person

places.name: Sōtō Zen Aarhus
places.notes: Sōtō Zen practice group in Aarhus

places.name: Aarhus Zendo (Øsal Ling)
places.notes: Teacher Denko John Mortensen; combines Zen and Dzogchen; included as hybrid Zen presence; also hosts Interbeing Denmark Plum Village days monthly

places.name: Interbeing Denmark (Plum Village Copenhagen Sangha)
places.notes: Plum Village tradition mindfulness sangha in Denmark; Tuesday evening meets at Det Åbne Rum or Naturcenter Vestamager; monthly mindfulness day at Chùa Liễu Quán Vietnamese temple in Glostrup, led by Br. Thay Phap Tru
